summary: Census register from an unknown site — a cadastral census counting individuals by geographic zone. 79 cords, 9 STRING (11%, 89% hits), 56 INT, 14 EMPTY. Architecture 2+-ARBRE, depth L3, 16 clusters. 5+ colors: W(17), AB(15), MB(12), KB(10), G0(8). Vocabulary (onset-corrected): kiki x2-3 (self, from chiki/chi), chiqa x1 (truth), qaqa x1 + qaqaqa x1 (rock + rock-TOPIC), taqa x1 (boundary), kaqa x1 (wall-AY), mama x1 (mother/Moon). kiki (self) dominant + qaqa + kaqa + taqa + chiqa = cadastral census like UR037. mama x1 in cadastral context = mother (lineage). qaqaqa = qaqa+qa (rock-TOPIC) = compound form like UR120's doubled forms.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  79 cords. 9 STRING (11%, 89% hits), 56 INT, 14 EMPTY.
  Architecture: 2+-ARBRE, depth L3. 16 clusters.
  Colors: W(17), AB(15), MB(12), KB(10), G0(8) — 5+ colors.
  Provenance: Unknown. Museum: Ethnologisches Museum, Berlin.

VOCABULARY (onset-corrected)
  kiki x2-3 (self/individual, from chiki/chi → kiki) — the counted person, DOMINANT
  chiqa x1 (truth) — verification
  qaqa x1 (rock/mountain) — geographic
  qaqaqa x1 (rock-TOPIC = qaqa+qa compound) — geographic, topicalized
  taqa x1 (boundary) — geographic
  kaqa x1 (wall, Aymara) — geographic
  mama x1 (mother/lineage) — kinship, not astronomical in this context

CLASSIFICATION
  kiki (self) dominant + qaqa + kaqa + taqa + chiqa = pure CADASTRAL CENSUS — the same signature as UR037 (Museo Amano). mama x1 in cadastral context = mother/lineage (not Moon). The tree architecture (2+-ARBRE, L3) distinguishes this from UR037's flat paired structure.

MORPHOLOGY
  qaqaqa = qaqa + qa (rock + TOPIC marker) — a compound form. Similar doubled/extended forms appear in UR120. The topic marker signals that the rock formation is the subject of administrative discussion.

CROSS-CORPUS
  UR204 vs UR037 (Museo Amano): Same vocabulary (kiki, qaqa, taqa, kaqa, chiqa). UR037 has 171 cords and flat structure; UR204 has 79 cords and tree structure (L3). Both count individuals by geographic zone. UR204 is smaller but deeper.
  UR204 vs UR120: Both contain extended/doubled forms (qaqaqa in UR204, doubled vocabulary in UR120).

NARRATIVE
  Self. That is the dominant word — *kiki*, the individual counted for the census, appearing two or three times in nine words of text. The rest is pure cadastral: rocks (*qaqa*), walls (*kaqa*), boundaries (*taqa*), truth (*chiqa*). A census register from an unknown site, held in Berlin, counting individuals by geographic zone — the same format as UR037 from the Museo Amano in Lima, but smaller and with a tree structure three levels deep.

  One word stands apart from the standard cadastral set: *mama* — mother, lineage. In an astronomical khipu this would mean the Moon. Here, surrounded by rocks and walls and boundaries, it means the maternal line — the kinship axis along which the census is organized. And one compound: *qaqaqa*, rock topicalized, the mountain that is the subject of administrative record. Not just a rock. The rock that matters.
references: Sivan 2026, ALBA Project — Census register analysis, onset correction chiki→kiki
Cross-corpus: UR037 (cadastral census, Museo Amano, kiki x11), UR120 (doubled forms)
